new_classes.label_en: PDO Still Wine in Containers of More Than 2 Litres (CN 22042928)
new_classes.label_de: Stillwein mit g.U. in Behältnissen von mehr als 2 Litern (KN 22042928)
new_classes.definition_en: Non-sparkling wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO), presented in containers holding more than 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2204 29 28 (Chapter 22). See Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) PDO (g.U.) status via someValuesFrom ProtectedDesignationOfOrigin, (2) container volume greater than 2 litres via a decimalRange minExclusive restriction on maxContainerVolumeL, and (3) the CN code 22042928 via a hasValue restriction on cnHeadingCode. The legal note also references Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, which defines the precise conditions for PDO wines — this note's detailed criteria (e.g. specific geographic areas, grape varieties, production methods) are not individually axiomatised here because they would require a separate, specialised modelling effort and are referenced by cross-reference only. The parent class StillWine already encodes the non-sparkling, grape-wine nature. The large-container (>2 L) discrimination from the sibling PDOStillWineSmallContainer is captured. Coverage is slightly below 1.0 because the precise content of Additional Note 6(a) is not fully axiomatised.
